heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 225 on 28 May 2026.

storm.status: The last storm was on 18 July 2026 which lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: We are in the midst of one of the longest dry periods recorded.  This dry period is ranked 1st since records began in 23 September 2024.  The current stretch started on 03 July 2026 and has lasted 20 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 3 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The largest temperature gap recorded is 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C) on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: A heatwave is active and has been ongoing for 22 days since 2026-07-01.  This year has seen 3 heatwaves, compared to an average of 1.9 per year.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 28 June 2026 and it has only happened 68 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (221°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: The top recorded wind gust is 128.2 mph on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The largest wind run ever was on 21 October 2012 with a value of 1737 miles.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 2.2°C) and outside temperature (up 1.5°C).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 565 m), dew point temperature (up 1.6°C), and inside humidity (up 6%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 285.9m lower than yesterday, 564.7m lower than the past week, 135.7m higher than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 1829m to a maximum of 6260m, averaging 3940m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 4500m and 3800m respectively.
cloud levels.summary: The sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine.

summary.summary: Inside; hot for most of the day. Outside; extreme heat is expected, potentially dangerous in the sun. Also, humidity will be quite oppressive and calm and still conditions are expected. Chance of light rain, the sky’s high clouds will permit plenty of sunshine, and very bright daylight. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
